content.full_text:
February 18, 1983

Ms. Shauna Clark  
City Clerk  
P.O. Box 1318  
San Bernardino, CA 92402

Re: Telephone Advice Request; Our File No. A-83-043

Dear Ms. Clark:

You telephoned to request advice regarding the interpretation of AB 1040 (Levine), Chap. 1049, Stats. 1982, Government Code Section 84308. You have requested that I forward to you general information regarding interpretation of the statute. I am enclosing our packet of regulations which recently became effective. You have also requested advice on a couple specific questions.

1. Is a directly elected mayor exempt under Section 84308(d)?

Yes. When 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1(a) and 18438.7(d) are read together, they provide that a directly elected mayor is excluded under Section 84308(d).

2. If city council sits together as the entire redevelopment agency, is that body exempt under Section 84308(d)?

Yes. Under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18348.1(b)(1), the redevelopment agency (or similarly constituted body) is exempt because they are sitting as the ex officio governing body of the district. This is not true when they mix with other officials.

3. Is a directly elected mayor exempt when sitting as the executive of the redevelopment agency, a role analogous to his role as mayor?

Ms. Shauna Clark  
February 18, 1983  
Page Two

Yes. He would be sitting ex officio and would be exempt under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18348.1(b)(1).

Should you have other questions, please do not hesitate to contact this office at 916/322-5901.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Robert E. Leidigh  
Counsel  
Legal Division

REL:km  
Enclosure
